qualifying_org_definition: Any nonprofit or organization where the recipient performs volunteer service; community or public service does not need to be for a formal organization (e.g., helping a neighbor counts). No MOU or provider registration required.
verification_instrument: BFA Form 320 (Reporting Monthly Participation in Community Engagement Activities) — self-attested monthly log signed by recipient, includes org name, contact person, and hours; alternatively submitted electronically via NH EASY portal.
hours_formula_detail: NH follows the federal 7 CFR 273.24 baseline of 80 hours per month (or 20 per week). Multiple NH DHHS sources and secondary sources confirm '80 hours per month' as the stated threshold; no workfare-allotment formula found.
accepts_outofstate_detail: NH policy does not restrict volunteer site location or require in-state registration. The qualifying org definition is exceptionally broad — informal volunteer service (e.g., helping a neighbor) counts, so formal nonprofit status is not even required. No source addresses out-of-state nonprofits specifically; under the federal default and NH's permissive framing, an out-of-state 501(c)(3) like Tended should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: None as of FY2026. A partial waiver covering only the towns of Stratford and Hale's Location was approved for October 1, 2024 – September 30, 2025 (based on unemployment rates 20% above the national average). That waiver expired September 30, 2025. No FY2026 waiver was filed or approved; OBBBA eliminated broad waiver authority and no NH towns currently meet the new 10% unemployment threshold. NH is fully enforced statewide as of 2026.
submission_deadline: Monthly; recipients must report within 10 days if hours fall below the 80-hour threshold (BFA Form 215 / FSM 245.05 language). BFA Form 320 is submitted monthly via NH EASY portal or paper.
notes:
NH is state-administered with no county variation. Volunteer hours self-reported via BFA Form 320 (or NH EASY portal); recipient signs the log and includes org name and contact — no EDP, county assignment, MOU, or provider registration found in any source. Confidence is medium rather than high because the primary DHHS policy manual pages (dhhs.nh.gov FSM) returned 403, and the BFA Form 320 PDF was not directly readable; the clean-lane characterization rests on consistent secondary evidence from somedoing.com and search-result snippets from NH state releases. SR 26-10 (May 2026) confirms ABAWD is actively enforced under OBBBA.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted four adversarial search angles specifically designed to refute the Tier 1 classification: (1) NH SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026/FY27 for volunteer-track gating; (2) ABAWD work registration/intake/case-manager requirement; (3) approved volunteer site list or provider registration; (4) legal aid and anti-hunger sources for hidden prestep. No evidence of a mandatory prestep was found. Key confirmations: BFA Form 320 is definitively confirmed as the self-attestation instrument via search snippet from SR 19-04 — the form is recipient-signed and includes org name, contact person, and hours, submitted via NH EASY or paper. FSM 245.05 snippet confirms unpaid volunteer work counts and recipient must 'document the actual hours worked' (self-documentation, no county-assignment language). NH SNAP E&T is confirmed entirely voluntary: 'New Hampshire operates a voluntary E&T program, all work registrants are exempt' — WorkNowNH enrollment is NOT a required prestep for volunteer hours to count. No approved-site list or nonprofit MOU/registration requirement was surfaced across all searches. The NHFB SNAP Outreach Partner Resources is for food pantry outreach participation, not host-site registration for ABAWD volunteer credits. FSM pages (245.05, 245.15, 809, SR 19-04, SR 24-26, SR 24-19, SR 23-26) continue to return HTTP 403 from dhhs.nh.gov, but their content is confirmed via search engine snippets quoting the documents directly. The 2025 SNAP E&T State Plan PDF (nheconomy.com) was retrieved but is binary-encoded and not extractable by WebFetch. Adversarial case failed: no hidden EDP, mandatory enrollment, approved-site list, or workfare-style registration found. Tier 1 classification stands. Confidence upgraded to HIGH: BFA Form 320 as the self-reported verification instrument is multi-source confirmed (SR 19-04 snippet, somedoing.com, FSM 245.05 snippet, NH EASY portal reference), the voluntary E&T structure eliminates the WorkNowNH-as-mandatory-prestep risk, and no counter-evidence was found across targeted adversarial searches.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: confirmed 80 hours/month via multiple NH DHHS secondary sources and the GlobalBenefits.org 2026 NH guide; federal_80 formula applies, no workfare-allotment calculation found. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: policy is 'silent' — NH's qualifying org definition is exceptionally permissive (informal volunteer service including helping a neighbor qualifies; no formal nonprofit status required), so there is no in-state restriction to find; federal default permissiveness applies. (3) Geographic waivers: FY2025 partial waiver (Stratford + Hale's Location, Oct 1 2024–Sep 30 2025) confirmed via extracted PDF from FNS (nh-abawd-response-fy2025.pdf); that waiver expired and no FY2026 renewal found; OBBBA eliminated broad waiver authority; NH is fully enforced statewide as of 2026. (4) Submission UX: BFA Form 320 submitted via NH EASY online portal or paper to county office; monthly cadence; 10-day reporting window if hours drop below threshold (BFA Form 215 language). (5) Form fields: BFA Form 320 includes org name, contact person (supervisor name), recipient name, hours total, hours by day/week, activity description, period dates, and recipient signature; no EIN or notarization requirement found in any source.
